gaming.title: Let's Play!
gaming.translation: Come on, let's play a game for a bit!

practice.question: What does 'जरा (Jara)' mean?

gaming.note: 'चल' is one of the most useful words in spoken Marathi — it's dropped at the start of almost any casual suggestion, not just gaming ones.

gaming.title: I Won!
gaming.sentence: अरे, मी जिंकलो! पुन्हा खेळायचं का?
gaming.transliteration: Are, mi jinkalo! Punha khelaayacha ka?
gaming.translation: Hey, I won! Want to play again?

practice.question: Which sentence correctly says "I won" for a female speaker?

gaming.note: Getting the gender ending right on past-tense verbs is one of the trickiest parts of spoken Marathi for learners — locals notice it immediately, so it's worth practicing with phrases you'll actually say.
gaming.gender_note: Past-tense verbs in Marathi change with the speaker's gender: '...लो' for male speakers, '...ले' for female speakers. The same pattern applies to most past-tense verbs, e.g. हरलो/हरले (lost), झालो/झाले (became).

gaming.title: Which Game Are You Playing?
gaming.translation: Which game are you playing these days?

practice.question: Which sentence correctly asks a female friend "Which game are you playing?"
options: तू कोणता गेम खेळतोस? (Tu konta game kheltos?)
तू कोणता गेम खेळतेस? (Tu konta game kheltes?)
तू कोणता गेम खेळतो? (Tu konta game khelto?)
तू कोणता गेम खेळते? (Tu konta game khelte?)
practice.answer: तू कोणता गेम खेळतेस? (Tu konta game kheltes?)

gaming.note: Among friends, 'तू' (informal you) is used naturally — switching to 'तुम्ही' would sound oddly formal for a casual gaming chat.
gaming.gender_note: Second-person verbs agree with the listener's gender: '...तोस' when speaking to a male friend, '...तेस' when speaking to a female friend.

gaming.title: Join My Team!
gaming.sentence: ये, माझ्या टीममध्ये जॉईन हो, सोबत खेळू!
gaming.transliteration: Ye, majhya team madhe join ho, sobat khelu!
gaming.translation: Come on, join my team, let's play together!

practice.question: Which Marathi word means "Friend" when referring to a girl?

gaming.note: Unlike English 'friend', Marathi has separate words depending on the friend's gender: मित्र for a male friend, मैत्रीण for a female friend. Mixing English words like 'टीम' and 'जॉईन' straight into Marathi sentences is completely normal in everyday speech.

gaming.title: My Favourite Game
gaming.sentence: हा माझा सगळ्यात आवडता गेम आहे.
gaming.transliteration: Ha majha saglyaat aavadta game aahe.
gaming.translation: This is my favourite game of all.

practice.question: What does 'आवडता (Aavadta)' mean?

gaming.note: 'आवडता' changes form depending on what it describes — आवडता गेम (favourite game), आवडती गोष्ट (favourite story), आवडतं गाणं (favourite song). The noun's gender decides the ending, not the speaker's.

gaming.title: I Leveled Up!
gaming.sentence: बघ, मी लेव्हल अप झालो, माझा स्कोअर पण वाढला!
gaming.transliteration: Bagh, mi level up zaalo, majha score pan vaadhla!
gaming.translation: Look, I leveled up, my score also increased!

practice.question: Which word correctly completes "मी लेव्हल अप ___" for a male speaker?

gaming.note: Words like 'लेव्हल' and 'स्कोअर' are almost never translated — Marathi gamers use the English terms directly inside Marathi sentences.
gaming.gender_note: झालो/झाले (became) follows the same male/female past-tense pattern you saw with जिंकलो/जिंकले.

gaming.title: Watching the Live Stream
gaming.sentence: तू माझा लाइव्ह स्ट्रीम बघतोयस का?
gaming.transliteration: Tu majha live stream baghtoyas ka?
gaming.translation: Are you watching my live stream?

practice.question: Which sentence correctly asks a male friend "Are you watching my live stream?"
options: तू माझा लाइव्ह स्ट्रीम बघतोयस का? (Tu majha live stream baghtoyas ka?)
तू माझा लाइव्ह स्ट्रीम बघतेयस का? (Tu majha live stream baghteyas ka?)
तू माझा लाइव्ह स्ट्रीम बघतोस का? (Tu majha live stream baghtos ka?)
तू माझा लाइव्ह स्ट्रीम बघते का? (Tu majha live stream baghte ka?)
practice.answer: तू माझा लाइव्ह स्ट्रीम बघतोयस का? (Tu majha live stream baghtoyas ka?)

gaming.note: Spoken Marathi loves contractions — 'बघत आहेस' shrinking to 'बघतोयस' is exactly the kind of shortcut native speakers use constantly but textbooks rarely teach.

gender_variants.marathi: तू माझा लाइव्ह स्ट्रीम बघतोयस का?
gender_variants.transliteration: Tu majha live stream baghtoyas ka?

gender_variants.marathi: तू माझा लाइव्ह स्ट्रीम बघतेयस का?
gender_variants.transliteration: Tu majha live stream baghteyas ka?

gaming.gender_note: 'बघतोयस/बघतेयस' is the casual spoken contraction of 'बघत आहेस' (you are watching) — you'll hear the short form far more often than the full one.

gaming.title: This Game Is Addictive
gaming.sentence: हा गेम म्हणजे एक नंबर टाइमपास आहे, वेळ कसा जातो कळतच नाही!
gaming.transliteration: Ha game mhanje ek number timepass aahe, vel kasa jaato kalatach nahi!
gaming.translation: This game is excellent time-pass, you don't even realize how time flies!

words.meaning: Casual fun / way to pass time

practice.question: What does the Marathi slang phrase "एक नंबर (Ek number)" mean?

gaming.note: 'एक नंबर' literally means 'number one' but is used as everyday slang for 'awesome.' 'टाइमपास' is a beloved Marathi-English hybrid word with no single English equivalent, but everyone instantly understands the vibe.

gaming.title: My Phone Is Lagging
gaming.sentence: अरे यार, नेट स्लो आहे आणि फोनही हँग होतोय!
gaming.transliteration: Are yaar, net slow aahe ani phone-hi hang hotoy!
gaming.translation: Ugh dude, the net is slow and my phone is hanging too!

practice.question: Where does the word "यार (Yaar)" come from, and what does it mean?

gaming.note: 'यार' comes from Hindi/Urdu but gets used constantly in casual spoken Marathi, especially among younger speakers in cities like Pune and Mumbai.

gaming.title: Good Game, Rematch Tomorrow!
gaming.sentence: गुड गेम भाऊ! उद्या रिमॅच पाहिजे.
gaming.transliteration: Good game bhau! Udya rematch paahije.
gaming.translation: Good game, bro! I want a rematch tomorrow.

practice.question: How is the word "भाऊ (Bhau)" commonly used among friends?
options: Casually like 'bro' in English, with no real sibling implied
practice.answer: Casually like 'bro' in English, with no real sibling implied

gaming.note: Among Marathi-speaking friends — especially young men — 'भाऊ' is used like 'bro' in English, with no actual sibling relationship implied. 'जीजी' (GG) is borrowed straight from international gaming culture and used exactly as in English.

anime.title: Have You Watched This Anime?
anime.sentence: तू हा अॅनिमे बघितला आहेस का?
anime.transliteration: Tu ha anime baghitla aahes ka?

practice.question: Which Marathi word means "Episode"?

anime.note: Notice 'बघितला' here agrees with the object 'अॅनिमे' (masculine), not the speaker's gender — Marathi past-tense transitive verbs follow the object, unlike intransitive verbs like जिंकलो/जिंकले.

anime.title: Which Character Do You Like?
anime.sentence: तुला सगळ्यात जास्त कोणतं कॅरेक्टर आवडतं?
anime.transliteration: Tula saglyaat jaast kontay character aavadta?
anime.translation: Which character do you like the most?

practice.question: What does "खलनायक (Khalnaayak)" mean?

anime.note: Most young fans say 'कॅरेक्टर' instead of the more formal native word 'पात्र' — borrowing the English term feels far more natural in casual fan talk.

anime.title: This Episode Was Amazing!

practice.question: Choose the correct meaning of "जबरी (Jabri)".

anime.note: 'जबरी' is everyday Marathi slang for 'awesome' or 'epic' — heard constantly in Pune and Mumbai casual speech, roughly equivalent to saying something is 'fire' in English slang.

anime.title: Waiting for the Next Season
anime.transliteration: Mi pudhachya season chi vaat baghtoy!
anime.translation: I'm waiting for the next season!

practice.question: The word "बघतोय (Baghtoy)" is a short spoken form of which full phrase?

anime.note: Spoken Marathi loves shortcuts — 'बघत आहे' almost always becomes 'बघतोय/बघतेय' in real conversation, just like the ...तोयस/...तेयस forms you've seen before.

gender_variants.transliteration: Mi pudhachya season chi vaat baghtoy!
Mi pudhachya season chi vaat baghtey!

anime.gender_note: बघतोय/बघतेय is the casual contraction of 'बघत आहे' (am watching/waiting) — male speakers use ...तोय, female speakers use ...तेय.

anime.title: Subbed or Dubbed?
anime.sentence: तू सबटायटल्ससोबत बघतोस की डब केलेला?
anime.transliteration: Tu subtitles sobat baghtos ki dub kelela?
anime.translation: Do you watch it with subtitles or the dubbed version?

practice.question: What does "डब केलेला (Dub kelela)" mean?

anime.note: Marathi/Hindi-dubbed anime is fairly rare, so most Indian fans default to subtitles — the 'sub vs dub' debate usually happens in this same English-Marathi mixed style.

gender_variants.marathi: तू सबटायटल्ससोबत बघतोस की डब केलेला?
gender_variants.transliteration: Tu subtitles sobat baghtos ki dub kelela?

gender_variants.marathi: तू सबटायटल्ससोबत बघतेस की डब केलेला?
gender_variants.transliteration: Tu subtitles sobat baghtes ki dub kelela?

anime.gender_note: Second-person present tense agrees with the listener's gender: ...तोस for a male friend, ...तेस for a female friend.

anime.title: This Episode Made Me Cry
anime.sentence: हा भाग बघून मी अगदी रडलो!
anime.transliteration: Ha bhaag baghun mi agadi radlo!
anime.translation: I literally cried watching this episode!

practice.question: Which sentence correctly says "I cried" for a female speaker?

anime.note: Crying over a sad anime ending is a shared, often half-joked-about experience among fans — saying 'मी अगदी रडलो/रडले' is a common, slightly dramatic way to express it.

gender_variants.marathi: हा भाग बघून मी अगदी रडलो!
gender_variants.transliteration: Ha bhaag baghun mi agadi radlo!

gender_variants.marathi: हा भाग बघून मी अगदी रडले!
gender_variants.transliteration: Ha bhaag baghun mi agadi radle!

anime.gender_note: रडलो/रडले follows the same intransitive past-tense pattern as जिंकलो/जिंकले — male speakers use ...लो, female speakers use ...ले.

anime.title: Binge-Watching All Night
anime.sentence: काल मी रात्रभर बिंज वॉच केलं, झोपच आली नाही!
anime.transliteration: Kaal mi raatrabhar binge watch kelay, zopach aali nahi!
anime.translation: Yesterday I binge-watched all night, I didn't even feel sleepy!

practice.question: What does "रात्रभर (Raatrabhar)" mean?

anime.note: 'बिंज वॉच करणे' is now used as a single borrowed verb phrase in casual Marathi, exactly like in English — often paired with native time words like रात्रभर or सलग.

anime.title: My Favourite Genre
anime.sentence: माझा आवडता प्रकार रोमान्स आणि अॅक्शन आहे.
anime.transliteration: Majha aavadta prakaar romance ani action aahe.
anime.translation: My favourite genre is romance and action.

practice.question: What does "विनोदी (Vinodi)" mean?

anime.note: 'प्रकार' is the natural Marathi word for 'genre/type' and works across anime, movies, and books alike — but the genre names themselves (रोमान्स, अॅक्शन) usually stay in English.

anime.title: Have You Read the Manga Too?
anime.sentence: तू मांगा पण वाचली आहेस का?
anime.transliteration: Tu manga pan vachli aahes ka?
anime.translation: Have you read the manga too?

practice.question: In the sentence "तू मांगा वाचली आहेस का?", the verb "वाचली" agrees with...

anime.note: Just like with अॅनिमे बघितला earlier, the verb here (वाचली) agrees with the object मांगा, not the speaker — a good reminder of how Marathi past-tense transitive verbs work.

anime.title: Let's Go to a Cosplay Event
anime.sentence: चल, एकदा कॉसप्ले इव्हेंटला जाऊ, मज्जा येईल!
anime.transliteration: Chal, ekda cosplay event la jau, majja yeil!
anime.translation: Come on, let's go to a cosplay event sometime, it'll be fun!

practice.question: What does "पोशाख (Poshakh)" mean?

anime.note: Anime conventions and cosplay meetups are growing in cities like Pune, Mumbai, and Bengaluru, where fans naturally mix English fandom terms into everyday Marathi conversation, just like in this lesson.
